title: Panhala Fort
tagline: Panhala Fort — heritage city of Maharashtra

overview.short: Panhala fort (also known as Panhalgad and Panhalla (literally "the home of serpents")), is located in Panhala, 20 kilometres northwest of Kolhapur in Maharashtra, India.
overview.description: Panhala fort (also known as Panhalgad and Panhalla (literally "the home of serpents")), is located in Panhala, 20 kilometres northwest of Kolhapur in Maharashtra, India. It is strategically located, looking over a pass in the Sahyadri mountain range which was a major trade route from Bijapur in the interior of Maharashtra to the coastal areas. Due to its strategic location, it was the centre of several skirmishes in the Deccan involving the Marathas, the Mughals and the British, the grandsons of Chhatrapati Shivaji Maharaj, the East India Company, the most notable battle being the Battle of Pa

howToReach.roadNote: Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

topPlaces.name: Siege of Panhala (1701)
topPlaces.description: The Siege of Panhala in 1701 was a significant military expedition led by Mughal emperor Aurangzeb during his campaign against the Marathas. The fort was under the command of Trimbakji, a Maratha leader who was later supported by Dhanaji Jadhav.

topPlaces.name: Siege of Panhala (1660)
topPlaces.description: The siege of Panhala was led by Siddi Jauhar on behalf of the Bijapur Sultanate, dispatched by Ali Adil Shah II to reclaim the Panhala Fort, was a momentous undertaking. Shivaji had seized Panhala on 28 November 1659, just 18 days after the death of Afzal Khan at Pratapgad.

topPlaces.name: Panhala
topPlaces.description: Panhala (Pronunciation: [pənʱaːɭa]) is a city and a Hill station Municipal Council (3177 feet above sea level) 18 km northwest of Kolhapur, in Kolhapur district in the Indian state of Maharashtra. Panhala is the smallest city in Maharashtra and being a Municipal Council the city is developing rapidly.

topPlaces.name: Masai Plateau
topPlaces.description: The Masai Plateau, also known as the Masai Pathar or Masai Sadas, is a plateau situated 30 kilometers west from Kolhapur city in Maharashtra. It is known for scenery and various types of seasonal wildflowers that bloom in the months of August and September.

topPlaces.name: Jyotiba Temple
topPlaces.description: Jyotiba Temple (Marathi: ज्योतिबा) is a holy site of Hinduism near Wadi Ratnagiri in Kolhapur district of Maharashtra state in western India. The deity of the temple is known by the same name.

topPlaces.name: Pohale Caves
topPlaces.description: Pohale Caves, also Pohala Caves or Pawala Caves, are a group of Buddhist caves located in Kolhapur District, Maharashtra, India, about 15 km northeast of Kolhapur. The caves are rather plain and were excavated in a rocky area near Jyotiba's hill.

items.note: The Siege of Panhala in 1701 was a significant military expedition led by Mughal emperor Aurangzeb during his campaign against the Marathas. The fort was under the command of Trimbakji, a Maratha leader who was later supported by Dhanaji Jadhav.

The siege of Panhala was led by Siddi Jauhar on behalf of the Bijapur Sultanate, dispatched by Ali Adil Shah II to reclaim the Panhala Fort, was a momentous undertaking. Shivaji had seized Panhala on 28 November 1659, just 18 days after the death of Afzal Khan at Pratapgad.

Panhala (Pronunciation: [pənʱaːɭa]) is a city and a Hill station Municipal Council (3177 feet above sea level) 18 km northwest of Kolhapur, in Kolhapur district in the Indian state of Maharashtra. Panhala is the smallest city in Maharashtra and being a Municipal Council the city is developing rapidly.

The Masai Plateau, also known as the Masai Pathar or Masai Sadas, is a plateau situated 30 kilometers west from Kolhapur city in Maharashtra. It is known for scenery and various types of seasonal wildflowers that bloom in the months of August and September.
Jyotiba Temple (Marathi: ज्योतिबा) is a holy site of Hinduism near Wadi Ratnagiri in Kolhapur district of Maharashtra state in western India. The deity of the temple is known by the same name.

Pohale Caves, also Pohala Caves or Pawala Caves, are a group of Buddhist caves located in Kolhapur District, Maharashtra, India, about 15 km northeast of Kolhapur. The caves are rather plain and were excavated in a rocky area near Jyotiba's hill.

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Panhala Fort?
faq.a: The best time to visit Panhala Fort is Oct – Mar. Summers are 26–40°C and winters are 12–28°C.

faq.q: How do I reach Panhala Fort?
faq.a: The nearest airport is Kolhapur Airport (25 km) and the nearest railway station is Vasco da Gama (Goa) (160 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Panhala Fort?
faq.a: 2 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Panhala Fort at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Panhala Fort?
faq.a: Stays in Panhala Fort start from ₹900 per night, with options across 4 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in Panhala Fort?
faq.a: The top attractions are Siege of Panhala (1701), Siege of Panhala (1660), Panhala, Masai Plateau, Jyotiba Temple.
